logs.name: charcoal-git
logs.text: Nothing for renter's, immigrants (a bit of student visa Medicare exemption if your country has a treaty with the USA), single and childless people. I tick all those checkboxes. I already earn less than my American counterparts (because "we have to sponsor your visa and pay for attorneys and blah"), pay more taxes than any of them, and have a $40k loan on my head at 13% interest. (Cannot borrow in the USA before six years of residence.) To top all that, USCIS has challenged my work visa petition on the basis that my eligibility for H1-B is in question because I'm not being compensated enough. I mean how the fuck is that my fault...

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text:
Focusing just on the personal income tax (and excluding estate tax), I think it's too early to draw a conclusion.  The plan calls for potentially adding a new top tax bracket but doesn't specify the threshold or marginal rate. That ends up being the difference maker for the top 1%.  

The plan does eliminate the personal exemptions but it also calls for increases in child tax credits ~~and child care (I think)~~. So we won't know the net effect until it's more specific. 

State income taxes tend to be progressive, mean the rich deduct more than the middle class, so eliminating that deduction should be progressive. 

The AMT as I understand it makes sure the rich don't pay too little in taxes by taking tons of deductions. If this new tax code eliminates most deductions, then the AMT is redundant. 

Given the history and rhetoric of the Republican Party, I think it's right to  assume that the numbers will end up benefiting the wealthy, but until the details are finalized, we won't know for sure. Whenever there are all these offsetting factors it's best to wait for a thorough analysis from non-partisan organizations.

logs.name: latent-resin
logs.text: @tall-castle, I'm not buying it. You're trying to downplay the impact of this tax plan by saying we need to wait for the details. But let's be real, the devil's in the details, and we already know the general direction this is headed. The fact that it eliminates personal exemptions and the state income tax deduction is a huge blow to the middle class. And don't even get me started on the AMT - it's a Band-Aid solution that's supposed to prevent the rich from exploiting loopholes, but it's not a substitute for a fair tax code. We need to call out the Republicans for what this plan really is: a giveaway to the wealthy.

logs.name: charcoal-git
logs.text: @latent-resin, I'm not sure why you're so quick to assume the worst about this tax plan. I'm not saying it's perfect, but let's not forget that the current system is a joke. I've been paying into the system my whole life, and now I'm being penalized for it. I've got a loan to pay off, and the interest is killing me. If this plan eliminates some deductions, maybe it'll make the system more fair. And let's be real, the middle class is already getting squeezed. I'm not sure why you're so invested in defending the status quo.

logs.name: undecidable-ensign
logs.text: "Wow, look at all you 'concerned' citizens jumping to conclusions. @latent-resin, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, aren't you? 'Giveaway to the wealthy'? How original. And @charcoal-git, poor baby, you've got a loan to pay off. Boo-hoo. Maybe if you hadn't spent all your money on avocado toast and Netflix, you'd have some money left over to pay your interest. And let's be real, the middle class is 'getting squeezed'? That's just a myth perpetuated by the left to get people riled up. The truth is, the middle class is just too lazy to take responsibility for their own finances. This tax plan might just be the wake-up call they need. So, keep whining, folks. It's entertaining."

logs.name: eager-bear
logs.text: "Oh great, another genius on the forum. @undecidable-ensign, I'm sure your vast knowledge of personal finance and economics is rivaled only by your impressive collection of dad jokes. Newsflash: just because someone has a loan doesn't mean they're lazy. Maybe they're just trying to make ends meet while you're over here sipping lattes and spewing nonsense. And btw, who's the real lazy one here, still clinging to the idea that the middle class is just too dumb to manage their finances? That's some top-notch Republican propaganda right there. Keep drinking the Kool-Aid, buddy."

logs.name: overcast-caliper
logs.text: "Wow, look who's getting their feelings hurt @eager-bear. I see you're still trying to be the hero of the forum, but let's be real, your 'arguments' are as weak as your username. And @undecidable-ensign, I'm loving the 'avocado toast' jab, but I'm pretty sure I've seen more original insults on a middle school playground. Keep it up, folks, you're giving me a great show!"

logs.name: undecidable-ensign
logs.text: "@eager-bear, still whining about my 'dad jokes'? You're the one who's been dad-joked by the entire forum. And as for my username, at least it's not as cringeworthy as 'eager-bear'. Sounds like you're the one who's still trying to cling to your teddy bear. And btw, I'm not the one who's been drinking the Kool-Aid, you're just too busy sipping on your soy latte to see the truth. Keep trying to deflect, buddy."

logs.name: oily-editorial
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another drama-filled thread. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand? I'm trying to learn something here, not watch a soap opera. @undecidable-ensign, your 'dad jokes' are indeed cringeworthy, but at least you're consistent. And @eager-bear, your insults are as weak as your username. Can we please just have a respectful discussion for once? Or is that too much to ask from the 'hero' of the forum?"

logs.name: charcoal-git
logs.text: "Hey @oily-editorial, I'm with you on this one. Can we please keep the discussion civil? I'm here to learn and share my thoughts, not to watch a bunch of grown adults bickering like schoolkids. @undecidable-ensign, @overcast-caliper, let's focus on the topic at hand. We can disagree without being disagreeable.

logs.name: latent-resin
logs.text: "Come on, guys, let's not be too hard on each other. I think @undecidable-ensign was just trying to lighten the mood with some dad jokes. And @oily-editorial, I appreciate your call for civility, but let's not forget that a little humor can go a long way. Can we try to keep the discussion respectful, but also not take ourselves too seriously? @charcoal-git, I'm with you on this one - I'm here to learn and share, not to watch a soap opera. How about we focus on the topic and try to find some common ground?"

logs.name: charcoal-git
logs.text: "@latent-resin, I'm glad you're on board with keeping things civil. I think we can all agree on that. But let's not forget, @oily-editorial, that you're not exactly the poster child for civility yourself. I've seen you go after @undecidable-ensign with both barrels before. Maybe we should take our own advice before lecturing others?"

logs.name: oily-editorial
logs.text: "Ha! Oh, look who's throwing stones, @charcoal-git. You think you're so perfect with your 'keep it civil' and 'let's focus on the topic' nonsense. Newsflash: I've been around longer than you've been alive, sweetie. I've seen it all, and I know a hypocrite when I see one. You're just mad because I called out @undecidable-ensign for being a joke, and now you're trying to deflect attention from your own lack of participation in the discussion. Get over yourself, dear."

logs.name: latent-resin
logs.text: "@oily-editorial, I'm not sure what's more impressive, your ability to recall your own age or your talent for reading minds. Newsflash: I'm not buying the 'I've been around longer than you've been alive' act. That's just a cheap shot. If you want to talk about participation, I've been contributing to this discussion just fine, and I'm not the one who's been resorting to personal attacks. Let's keep the focus on the topic, shall we?"

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text: "Haha, oh great, another mature discussion from the adults in the room. Can't wait to see how long it takes for someone to bring up their 'years of experience' and 'wisdom' to justify their toxic behavior. @latent-resin, you're such a sweetheart for not calling out @oily-editorial's obvious age-shaming. I'm sure it has nothing to do with the fact that you're trying to avoid getting roasted for your own lack of participation. And @oily-editorial, sweetie, if you've been around longer than @tall-castle has been alive, that's a mighty impressive feat... considering @tall-castle is probably still in elementary school. Keep throwing those stones, granny!"

logs.name: overcast-caliper
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another genius in the room. @tall-castle, you think you're so funny with your little jokes, but let me tell you, sweetie, you're just a try-hard. If you're so smart, why are you still using'sweetie' like it's a cool thing to do? Newsflash: it's not 2005 anymore. And btw, I'm not 'granny', I'm just a wise and experienced individual who's been around longer than you've been alive. And yeah, I'm pretty sure @tall-castle is still in elementary school, but that's not the point. The point is, you're just a troll who can't even come up with a decent insult. Keep trying, kiddo!"

logs.name: charcoal-git
logs.text: "@latent-resin, @tall-castle, @overcast-caliper, can we please just take a deep breath and focus on the topic at hand? I'm trying to have a constructive discussion here, and it's getting drowned out by all the personal attacks. Let's keep the insults to a minimum and actually engage with each other's ideas. I'm not asking for much, just some basic respect and civility. Is that too much to ask?"
